_comment: Project configuration for document classification. Copy to your project directory and fill in.

inclusion_criteria.actor: Who or what must be the subject? (e.g., 'a foreign government', 'a company', 'a regulator')
inclusion_criteria.action: What type of item qualifies? (e.g., 'policy measure', 'risk factor', 'commitment', 'allegation')
inclusion_criteria.scope: What is in/out of scope? (e.g., 'current items only', 'all historical items')

exclusion_criteria: Items that do not match any theme definition
Passages that mention a keyword without describing a substantive item
unit_of_analysis: One distinct item per entry. Multiple passages about the same item = one entry. Different items = separate entries.

output_fields._comment: Define the fields each entry should contain. The core fields (entry_id, theme_key, section, exact_quote, description, match_strength) are always present. Add project-specific fields below.

audit_dimensions._comment: Define what to check during the audit-entries skill. Each dimension is a yes/no question applied to every entry.
audit_dimensions.category_match: Does this entry fall within the theme's specific domain?
audit_dimensions.quote_fidelity: Is the exact_quote truly verbatim from the source document?

counting_rules._named_dedup_note: Deduplicate named items by name within each section-category pair.
counting_rules._unnamed_dedup_note: Unnamed items have no unique identifier; count each invocation separately.

_sections_note: Leave empty to process all sections. Or list specific sections to focus on.
_taxonomy_note: List paths to any reference taxonomy files (e.g., classification codes, sector lists). Claude will consult these during extraction.
